description.value:
Where creation ends, Alyrax begins.

He is called many things — the Unmaker, the Endless Destruction, He Who Tears Down — but his followers simply call him the Answer. To them, destruction is not an end but a correction. A mercy. All things that are built will fall. Alyrax simply refuses to pretend otherwise.

His domain is not chaos, and that is what makes him dangerous. Alyrax does not destroy blindly — he chooses. Cities. Legacies. People. He picks apart the things others hold most dear with a deliberateness that feels almost personal, as if each act of ruin is a message meant for someone who never reads it.

His edicts are simple: tear down what stands. Finish what you start. Show no mercy once the decision to destroy has been made.

Sages debate whether Alyrax is truly evil or simply ancient in a way that has curdled into something terrible. His followers claim he is the only honest god — the one who acknowledges that nothing lasts. His enemies say there is a grief in his destruction that he has never learned to name.

Both may be right. Neither makes him less dangerous.
